column1: There are some nice waterfalls to the  southeast of town.  They fill Mirror Lake, which is lovely, but icy cold all year round.
column2: 「鎮南方有幾道漂亮的瀑布。它們注入鏡湖，景色宜人，不過一年四季都冷得要命。」

column1: Caspar at the store would like to be an adventurer, or so he says. He spends too much time dreaming and not enough doing, I think.
column2: 「店裡的卡斯帕說他想成為冒險者。我看他光做夢的時間太多，真正付諸行動的太少了。」

column1: Goblins are not tough, but when they gang up, they can take out an unwary adventurer.
column2: 「哥布林單隻不難對付，但當它們聚在一起時，就能幹掉不小心的冒險者。」

column1: A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  The Sheriff's Otto and Crusher at the tavern are both Goons.
column2: 「古恩有點像食人妖，不那麼強悍，但聰明一點。治安官的奧圖和客棧裡的克拉什都是古恩。」

column1: This is where an adventurer can find out who needs someone brave and courageous.  There are many jobs on the bulletin board over there.  It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.
column2: 「這是冒險者能找到誰需要勇敢者的地方。那邊的布告欄上有許多工作。冷天下午也是談論冒險故事的好地方。」

column1: We used to play cards here once a week as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died from monsters or brigands, or they just got too old.
column2: 「我們也曾每週在這裡打一次牌，但史畢柏格裡冒險者太少了。他們都死在怪物或山賊手下，要不就老到不行了。」

column1: He wasn't much of an adventurer, but he did manage to kill a Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with a Troll.
column2: 「他不是什麼偉大的冒險者，但他確實殺過一隻索魯斯，可惜他後來犯了個錯誤，惹上了巨魔。」

column1: Her house is just north of the town gate.  She's a nice woman.
column2: 「她的房子在城鎮大門北邊。她是個好女人。」

column1: A real Hero is someone who didn't start out strong or powerful, but uses his courage and brains and skills to become the best he can be.
column2: 「真正的英雄不是天生就強大，而是用勇氣、頭腦和技能來成為最好的自己。」

column1: I don't know much about magic, but the woman who runs that place scares me.
column2: 「我對魔法不太了解，但經營那地方的女人把我嚇到了。」

column1: Those weird things are magical.  They are best avoided.
column2: 「那些怪東西是有魔法的。最好別碰它們。」

column1: The Farmer's Mart is run by the Centaurs, Heinrich Pferdefedern and his daughter, Hilde.
column2: 「農夫市集由半人馬海因里希·佩德費德恩和他的女兒希爾德管理。」

column1: I am supposed to keep track of all the adventurers and make sure they sign their names in the log.  There was a time when I would go out and rescue foolhardy adventurers who got into too much trouble, but I don't get out much now.
column2: 「我得追蹤所有冒險者，確保他們在日誌上簽名。曾經有段時間我會出去救被困的冒險者，但現在我很少出門了。」

column1: Cheetaurs are ferocious creatures that charge out of nowhere and try to tear you to shreds.  Sometimes they succeed.
column2: 「獵豹人是兇悍的傢伙，會從天而降，想把你撕成碎片。有時候它們真的會成功。」

column1: You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls.  There are Trolls, Griffins, Cheetaurs, Mantrays, and Goblins wandering in the woods.
column2: 「你在牆上可以看到這一帶棲息的怪物。巨魔、獅鷲、獵豹人、曼特雷和哥布林在森林裡遊蕩。」

column1: I hear there are even some Ogres and Saurus Rexes back in this valley. There was a time when Schultz and I had gotten rid of most of the monsters around here, but we are just too old for that now.
column2: 「我聽說山谷裡還有食人妖和巨型索魯斯。從前舒爾茨和我清除了這一帶大部分的怪物，但現在我們年紀太大了。」

column1: That was the most vicious moose I ever saw.  Nearly bit my nose off.
column2: 「那是我見過最兇的麋鹿。差點咬掉我的鼻子。」

column1: The Mountains surrounding this valley are very high.  Snow covers the tops all year long.  The only way out of Spielburg Valley is through the pass, though I hear that it is blocked by snow again.
column2: 「環繞山谷的群山非常高聳。山頂一年到頭都被積雪覆蓋。離開史畢柏格山谷的唯一途徑是經過山口，不過我聽說又被雪封住了。」

column1: I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, the Guild Master of this hall.
column2: 「我是沃爾夫剛·阿班圖爾，這個大廳的公會總長。」

column1: Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though.
column2: 「食人妖類似古恩，但更兇殘。不過沒那麼聰明。」

column1: I like Otto, even if he is a Goon.  Schultz has him almost trained.
column2: 「我喜歡奧圖，雖然他是個古恩。舒爾茨差不多把他馴服了。」

column1: You need to talk to either the Baron or the Healer about that.
column2: 「關於這事，你得和男爵或治療師談談。」

column1: Don't mistake those things for Sauruses.   When they come at you, it is sometimes best just to run away.
column2: 「別把那些東西誤認為索魯斯。它們來襲時，有時候跑才是上策。」

column1: I remember when Hans killed the one there.  It was a tough fight, for him.
column2: 「我記得漢斯在那裡殺過一隻。對他來說那是場硬仗。」

column1: Schultz Meistersson and I have been friends for a long time.  He, too, was once a real adventurer.  Now he is just the Sheriff of this town.
column2: 「舒爾茨·邁斯特松和我是老友。他也曾是真正的冒險者。現在他只是這個鎮的治安官。」

column1: Now, there was a hero, and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg. He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago and his horse returned with large claw marks on it.  No sign Barnard's body was ever found.
column2: 「說到英雄，巴納德·馮·史畢柏格就算得上名副其實。五年前某個早上他騎馬去打獵，回來的只有馬，馬身上還有大爪痕。從未找到巴納德的屍體。」

column1: Spielburg Town is small now that most people have been driven off by the Brigands.  This is such a pretty place, too, with the mountains, forests, and waterfalls.
column2: 「史畢柏格鎮現在很小，大多數人都被山賊趕跑了。這本來是個漂亮的地方，有山、森林和瀑布。」

column1: I have never liked that place.  The bartender smiles all the time, but he is not very friendly.  Crusher lives up to his name.  He is nasty even for a Goon.
column2: 「我從沒喜歡過那地方。酒保整天都在笑，但不太友善。克拉什活得起他的名字。即使是古恩，他也特別凶惡。」

column1: They said they were from some place called Andromeda.  Must be someplace south of here, I guess.  They certainly were odd.
column2: 「他們說來自叫安卓美達的地方。大概在這兒南邊吧。他們確實很古怪。」

column1: This town used to be a thriving city before the brigands drove most of the people off.
column2: 「這座鎮在山賊趕走大多數人之前，曾是個繁榮的城市。」

column1: Trolls are very tough.  Fortunately, they only come out at night.  The woods are very dangerous at night.
column2: 「巨魔非常強悍。幸好它們只在夜間出沒。森林在夜間非常危險。」

column1: Schultz and I killed most of the monsters on those walls.
column2: 「舒爾茨和我殺了牆上大部分怪物。」

column1: The Saurus is a monster easy to avoid and easy to kill for a skilled adventurer.
column2: 「索魯斯是種容易躲避、技巧高超的冒險者容易對付的怪物。」

column1: I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the results on the wall.
column2: 「我記得我殺死一隻的那天。那是我見過最大的。它打得很兇，但我更兇。你可以在牆上看到戰績。」

column1: There are some nice waterfalls to the southeast of town.  They fill Mirror Lake, which is lovely, but icy cold all year round.
column2: 「鎮南方有幾道漂亮的瀑布。它們注入鏡湖，景色宜人，不過一年四季都冷得要命。」

column1: Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa.
column2: 「尤瑞克是個有趣的小個子，但很勇敢。他發誓要用一生去尋找男爵的艾爾莎。」

column1: A pile of wood is stacked against the wall.
column2: 一堆木頭靠著牆堆放。

column1: The firewood will serve more purpose here than in your pack.
column2: 柴火在這兒比在你背包裡用處更大。

column1: The knitting bag is decorated on the outside with knitted figures.  The Little Old Lady must do knitting when she's not asleep.
column2: 編織袋外面裝飾著編織人物。小老太太沒睡覺時定在織東西。

column1: This is not the best place to practice dagger throwing.
column2: 這不是練習匕首投擲的好地方。

column1: The birdcage is on a tall brass stand.  There is a cover draped over it to keep the bird warm and quiet.
column2: 鳥籠放在高的黃銅架上。上面蓋著布以保持鳥溫暖和安靜。

column1: Who would think a little birdie could be so noisy!
column2: 誰能想到一隻小鳥能這麼吵！

column1: CHIRP! CHIRP!
column2: 唧唧！唧唧！

column1: You quickly cover the cage and hope the bird shuts up.
column2: 你迅速蓋住籠子，希望鳥能閉嘴。

column1: You hear a voice from upstairs saying, "Kitty, you'd better not be bothering the little birdie again!"
column2: 你聽到樓上有聲音說，「小貓，你最好別再去惹小鳥了！」

column1: Little Birdie
column2: 小鳥

column1: Little Old Lady
column2: 小老太太

column1: You take the silver candlesticks and stow them in your pack.
column2: 你拿起銀燭臺並把它們放進背包。

column1: The candlesticks are heavy, ornate and made of sterling silver.
column2: 燭臺很沉，裝飾華麗，由純銀製成。

column1: You have a bad feeling about the very deep, low growl emanating from the cat.
column2: 你對貓發出的低沉、深重咆哮有種不祥的感覺。

column1: This is a very insistent cat. You give it one of your rations.
column2: 這是隻很堅持的貓。你給它一份你的口糧。

column1: You feed the nice kitty a crumb.
column2: 你給可愛的小貓一點麵包屑。

column1: The cat really likes being fed.  You give it a leftover morsel.
column2: 貓真的喜歡被餵食。你給它一點剩菜。

column1: You have nothing substantial to feed the cat.
column2: 你沒有什麼實質的東西可以餵貓。

column1: You pet the nice kitty.
column2: 你撫摸可愛的小貓。

column1: The cat really likes being petted.
column2: 貓真的喜歡被撫摸。

column1: This is a very insistent cat.
column2: 這是隻很堅持的貓。

column1: The little house cat seems harmless, but strangely restless.
column2: 小家貓似乎無害，但詭異地不安。

column1: The chair looks singularly uncomfortable.
column2: 椅子看起來特別不舒服。

column1: How does she keep those candles lit all night long?
column2: 她怎麼把那些蠟燭點著整晚？

column1: The chest is filled with old quilts, doilies, and sweaters five sizes too large for anyone to wear.  You really don't want any of them, so you close the chest again.
column2: 箱子裡裝滿了舊被子、蕾絲墊圈和大五號的毛衣，沒人穿得了。你真的不想要任何東西，所以你又關上箱子。

column1: A large chest sits beside the bureau.
column2: 一個大箱子放在櫥櫃旁邊。

column1: KITTY!!!
SQUEEEEAK!
column2: 吱呀呀呀呀！

column1: CREEEEAAK!
column2: 吱嘎呀呀呀！

column1: The owner of the house awakens...
column2: 房子的主人醒了……

column1: Help!  Burglars!  Sheriff!  Help!  Kitty!  Kitty!
column2: 「救命！小偷！治安官！救命！小貓！小貓！」

column1: SCRAAAAWK!
column2: 「呱呀呀呀呀！」

column1: You find 3 silvers that have gotten caught in the cracks.
column2: 你找到了 3 個銀幣卡在裂縫裡。

column1: You find nothing else by searching the couch.
column2: 你在沙發上搜索沒找到其他東西。

column1: The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your maiden aunt's.
column2: 沙發看起來和你未婚姑母的一樣不舒服。

column1: You find a single silver in one of the desk drawers.  You find nothing else of any value to you.
column2: 你在辦公桌的某個抽屜裡找到一個銀幣。你沒找到其他對你有價值的東西。

column1: The stairs lead to the door to an upstairs room.
column2: 樓梯通向樓上房間的門。

column1: Against the wall, there is a large wooden desk with many drawers.
column2: 靠著牆有一個大木桌，有很多抽屜。

column1: The fire is out, but you can still feel the heat from the hearth.
column2: 火熄了，但你仍能感到壁爐的熱。

column1: It's a hurricane lamp.  Funny, you've never heard of hurricanes in Spielburg.
column2: 這是颶風燈。有趣的是，你從沒在史畢柏格聽說過颶風。

column1: The light of the hurricane lamp.
column2: 颶風燈的光。

column1: Nothing but dirt and leaves there.
column2: 那兒只有灰塵和樹葉。

column1: It's a Shrubbery!
column2: 那是灌木叢！

column1: The Little Old Lady left her purse on the couch.  Mighty careless of her.
column2: 小老太太把錢包留在沙發上。她真粗心。

column1: That'll teach you to play daggers with a cat!
column2: 那就是你和貓玩匕首的下場！

column1: Oh, Oh!  Looks like you've blundered into the cat's territory.
column2: 哎呀！看起來你誤入了貓的領地。

column1: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.
column2: 當你走進去時，薰衣草和灰塵的味道充滿了你的鼻子。這讓你想起你曾經去過的姑母的房子。

column1: There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.
column2: 樓梯附近有一個蓋著的鳥籠，沙發旁邊有一個編織籃。

column1: A string of pearls seems to have fallen into the bag among the knitting.  You take the pearls, of course.  (The knitting you can do without.)
column2: 一串珍珠似乎掉進了編織物中的袋子裡。你當然拿走了珍珠。（編織物你可以不要。）

column1: You find nothing else of value in the desk.
column2: 你在桌子裡沒找到其他有價值的東西。

column1: In the purse, you find 20 silvers and some soiled hankies.  You take the silver.
column2: 在錢包裡，你找到 20 個銀幣和一些髒手帕。你拿走銀幣。

column1: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.
column2: 當你走進去時，薰衣草和灰塵的味道充滿了你的鼻子。這讓你想起你曾經去過的姑母的房子。

column1: The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your aunt's.  There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.
column2: 沙發看起來和你姑母的一樣不舒服。樓梯附近有一個蓋著的鳥籠，沙發旁邊有一個編織籃。

column1: The squared rug matches the walls.  It appears the floor has recently been painted.
column2: 方形地毯和牆相配。地板似乎剛被粉刷過。

column1: It's not locked.  In fact, the lock is broken.
column2: 沒有鎖。實際上，鎖壞了。

column1: It's a large, wooden chest, undoubtedly filled with priceless treasures.
column2: 這是一個大的木製箱子，無疑裝滿了無價之寶。

column1: It's not locked.  In fact, the lock is broken.
column2: 沒有鎖。實際上，鎖壞了。

column1: There is an assortment of plastic fruit on the old wooden table.
column2: 舊木桌上有各種塑膠水果。

column1: The wood on this table has been polished into total submission.  A lace doily guards it from dust and water rings.
column2: 這張桌子的木頭被拋光得發亮。蕾絲墊圈保護它不被灰塵和水漬傷害。

column1: You have a weird feeling that someone is watching you, and you decide you'd better not touch anything.
column2: 你有種奇怪的感覺，有人在盯著你，所以你決定最好別碰任何東西。

column1: Accoutrements and equipment.
column2: 裝飾品和器材。

column1: This mysterious orb feels as smooth as glass, and icy cold to the touch.
column2: 這個神祕的球體摸起來光滑如玻璃，冷得像冰。

column1: This strange globe radiates with an eerie light.
column2: 這個奇異的球體散發著詭異的光。

column1: That's way out of reach.
column2: 那遠遠超出範圍。

column1: It's not a pretty sight.
column2: 這不是好看的景象。

column1: You can only guess at the original contents of this bottle since it is now empty.
column2: 既然瓶子現在是空的，你只能猜測它原本的內容物。

column1: It's a blue bottle of blue bottle flies.
column2: 這是裝著藍瓶蒼蠅的藍色瓶子。

column1: Since you don't have time to read, you leave them.
column2: 因為你沒時間讀，你把它們留下了。

column1: The titles are totally unfamiliar to you.  The books dealing with sorcery, necromancy and other occult subjects.
column2: 標題對你完全陌生。這些書涉及巫術、死靈法術和其他神祕主題。

column1: Not knowing what the potions are, they will do you no good.
column2: 不知道藥水是什麼，它們對你沒有用處。

column1: Bottles of mephitic potions.
column2: 充滿惡臭的藥水瓶。

column1: Afraid that the contents might jump out at you, you leave the jar alone.
column2: 害怕內容物會跳出來，你不敢動這個罐子。

column1: A brown jar of crushed cockroach essence.
column2: 一個裝著碾碎蟑螂精華的棕色罐子。

column1: The ceiling is shrouded with clouds of smoke.
column2: 天花板被烈烈升起的煙霧籠罩。

column1: You feel like you're floating on air.
column2: 你感覺自己在飄浮。

column1: As your hand nears the coals, you discover that they are burning.
column2: 當你的手靠近煤炭時，你發現它們在燃燒。

column1: The coals are glowing.
column2: 煤炭在發光。

column1: You don't know how to use a crystal ball.
column2: 你不知道怎麼用水晶球。

column1: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of wizardry.
column2: 水晶球，只對懂得巫術之道的人有用。

column1: Whether it's because that thing is too far to reach, has sharp, nasty claws, and wicked teeth, or because it glares at you, you decide you'd better not touch it.
column2: 不管是因為那東西太遠夠不著、有尖銳可怕的爪子和兇惡的牙齒，還是因為它瞪著你，你決定最好別碰它。

column1: It looks like a sculpture of some weird, bat-winged creature.
column2: 它看起來像某種怪異的蝙蝠翅膀生物的雕像。

column1: The bat-winged creature must be some sort of familiar.  There is an uncanny look in its eyes, and you don't want to look at it too closely.  It is clearly magical.
column2: 蝙蝠翅膀生物應該是某種魔寵。它的眼睛有種詭異的光，你不想太仔細看它。它顯然是有魔法的。

column1: It's not familiar enough with you to want to chat.
column2: 它和你還不夠熟，不想聊天。

column1: On closer observation, you discover that they cute little fish are piranhas.  You wisely keep your fingers out.
column2: 仔細看，你發現那些可愛的小魚是食人魚。你明智地把手指遠遠放著。

column1: It's a bowl of pretty little fish.
column2: 這是一碗漂亮的小魚。

column1: The floor has an oily, glassy feel to it.
column2: 地板摸起來油膩、有玻璃感。

column1: The floor is almost completely covered by a strange symbol.
column2: 地板幾乎完全被一個奇異的符號覆蓋。

column1: Don't touch -- that stuff looks corrosive.
column2: 別碰——那東西看起來是腐蝕性的。

column1: The jar is filled with a corrosive-looking green substance.
column2: 罐子裡裝滿了看起來腐蝕性的綠色物質。

column1: You'll never get a head that way!  Besides, you don't really need a face-lift yet.
column2: 那樣你永遠成不了大人物！而且，你還真的不需要拉皮手術。

column1: A replacement part for a magical face-lift.
column2: 魔法拉皮手術的替換零件。

column1: It's a jar of dried Mantray jerky.  Yum!  (Not.)
column2: 這是一罐乾曼特雷肉乾。好吃！（其實不然。）

column1: Don't touch the mortar -- you'd be terribly embarrassed if you made it accidentally fire.
column2: 別碰杵臼——如果你不小心讓它發射，會超級尷尬。

column1: A mortar, complete with the traditional pestle.
column2: 完整的杵臼。
